commit.message:
fix(queue): Add diagnostics for silent thread drops (#92)

Add queue-routing diagnostics for silent Slack thread follow-up drops.

The production symptoms here were hard to separate because some messages
disappeared before any visible Slack acknowledgement, while others
reached the subscribed-thread classifier and failed closed on schema
parsing. This change makes the ingress and callback no-op paths explicit
in logs so we can distinguish duplicate suppression, passive no-reply
decisions, and queue-processing skips.

It also removes the subscribed-thread classifier reason-length cap so
otherwise valid classifier responses do not turn into
ai_completion_schema_parse_failed and subscribed_reply_classifier_failed
warnings.

This keeps behavior the same for successful paths while making the
failure modes observable enough to debug the next incident.

---------

Co-authored-by: GPT-5 <noreply@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add local-first workflow execution to the orchestrator (#43) closes #42 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | Orchestrator advancement depended on the remote tracker path plus `WORKFLOW.md` prompt/config loading. | Orchestrator can load local issue project config, scan local issue trees, evaluate `workflow.yaml` transitions, and advance state from local files. | | Observable effect | There was no local-first adapter that could move issues between `ready/`, `in-progress/`, and `review/` based on workpad intent and DSL conditions. | A local tracker path now discovers local issues, applies `when` checks, moves files, updates frontmatter, preserves workpad/artifacts, and dispatches the `siaan-inprogress` prompt template from local files. | | Affected inputs | Tracker configuration assumed remote-backed issue state and a single workflow prompt source. | Tracker config now accepts `kind = local` plus local config/project settings; local workflow DSL and `issue.md` / `workpad.md` become the execution inputs for the local-first path. | ```mermaid stateDiagram-v2 [*] --> Ready Ready --> InProgress: declared transition / adapter update InProgress --> Review: workpad status + when checks pass InProgress --> Ready: workpad status + when checks pass ``` ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: Existing orchestrator behavior still runs through the same tracker/prompt entry points, skills only write `workpad.md`, and state changes happen only after transition checks pass. - **Explicitly unchanged**: GitHub adapter/client behavior and existing tracker-facing orchestration surfaces remain intact; the new path is additive under `tracker.kind = local`. - **Out of scope**: Full remote-origin adapter handoff proof, additional stage-skill extraction beyond `siaan-inprogress`, and local issue -> remote issue sync. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | Local workflow parsing and transition semantics regress OR/AND/order behavior | `make -C elixir all` | N/A (local command) | | Local adapter breaks tracker compatibility or prompt rendering | `make -C elixir all` | N/A (local command) | | Local-origin handoff path does not advance into `in-progress` | Disposable proof via `SymphonyElixir.Local.Adapter.fetch_candidate_issues/0` and `update_issue_state/2` produced `status:ready -> status:in-progress` | N/A (local command) | | `siaan-inprogress` cannot operate within the file permission boundary | Real `codex exec` proof updated only `workpad.md` and left `issue.md` unchanged | N/A (local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: Local state transitions could mis-handle file layout differences between single-file states and directory-backed states. - **Blast radius**: `SymphonyElixir.Local.*`, tracker config loading, prompt rendering, and tests around the status dashboard / prompt builder touched during gate cleanup. - **How to detect**: `make -C elixir all` failure, missing local candidates, incorrect frontmatter state after a transition, or a skill prompt missing local file paths. - **How to rollback**: Revert commit `ebcda59` to remove the local tracker path and restore the previous remote-only behavior. ### Review Focus 1. Check that `SymphonyElixir.Local.Adapter` is the only code path performing local file moves/frontmatter state changes after transition checks. 2. Check that `SymphonyElixir.Local.Workflow` preserves the DSL semantics: AND within `when`, OR across repeated transitions, and first-match-wins ordering. 3. Check that prompt rendering and issue metadata stay backward compatible while enabling local skill templates.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) No L1 change in deployed system boundaries; this PR changes the orchestrator's internal source of truth and skill handoff path. ### Container (C4-L2) The Elixir orchestrator container/process now has an internal local-tracker execution path alongside the existing remote adapters. No new deployable container is introduced. ### Component (C4-L3) ```mermaid flowchart LR Config[Config / Tracker settings] --> LocalProject[Local.ProjectConfig] LocalProject --> LocalWorkflow[Local.Workflow] LocalWorkflow --> LocalIssue[Local.Issue] LocalIssue --> LocalAdapter[Local.Adapter] LocalAdapter --> PromptBuilder[PromptBuilder] PromptBuilder --> SkillTemplate[siaan-inprogress template] LocalAdapter --> Workpad[issue.md / workpad.md files] ``` ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - `Tracker` -> `tracker_module/0` -> local adapter dispatch in `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/tracker.ex` - `Config` / `Config.Schema` -> local tracker fields in `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/config.ex` and `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/config/schema.ex` - `Local.Adapter` -> `fetch_candidate_issues/0`, `update_issue_state/2`, `maybe_apply_transition/2` - `Local.Workflow` -> `load/1`, `first_matching_transition_to/5` - `PromptBuilder` -> local prompt-template path handling in `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/prompt_builder.ex` ### Decision Record - **Decision**: Introduce a dedicated `SymphonyElixir.Local.*` adapter stack rather than overloading the existing GitHub/Linear adapters. - **Alternatives considered**: Extend the existing remote adapter types with local-file branches, or keep the local-first path embedded in `WORKFLOW.md`-centric logic. - **Trade-offs**: Adds new modules and tests, but keeps local-first behavior isolated and makes transition semantics explicit in one place. - **Why chosen**: The issue requires the orchestrator core to stop depending on direct remote tracker advancement while preserving existing orchestration entry points. - **Implementation links**: `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/local/adapter.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/local/workflow.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/local/issue.ex`, `elixir/priv/skills/siaan-inprogress.md`

commit.message:
fix: handle Supabase v2 update builder without select

- remove unsupported .select() from update() chains in project_store
- update regression tests to assert update chains do not call select
- move BYOK table migration into supabase/migrations as 008_user_llm_keys.sql

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(dbus): proxy session bus access with xdg-dbus-proxy

Add a dedicated DBus proxy stage that starts xdg-dbus-proxy on the host, creates a per-session filtered socket under the NAS runtime dir, and mounts that runtime into the container as /run/user/$UID. The container now points DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS at the proxy socket instead of bind-mounting the host session bus directly.

This also adds profile-level dbus.session configuration, validation, merge behavior, mount/env wiring, and focused tests for config parsing, mount injection, and proxy stage lifecycle. README now documents the new general DBus session proxy feature and includes a Secret Service example.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: order Supabase update.select before filters

- fix project update chains to call select() before eq()/in_() in project_store
- add regression tests asserting select-before-filter ordering for update paths

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(launch): handle Ctrl-C as interrupt during docker run

When nas was waiting on an interactive docker run, pressing Ctrl-C could tear down the hostexec broker before the child process finished unwinding. If a hostexec request was still pending, that surfaced as an ordinary error path instead of a normal interrupt exit.

This change routes docker run through a signal-aware helper that forwards SIGINT and SIGTERM to the child process and reports an explicit interrupted state. The CLI now exits with code 130 for that case, and a focused docker client test covers the interrupt path to prevent regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>
